mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment rates signal labor market strength, leading to higher consumer spending and corporate earnings, positive for S&P 500 over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines are not followed by positive forward returns in 60-day windows (Rank IC < 0 over rolling 3-year periods), hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in headline CPI at or above 0.3% signal rising inflation that compels the Fed to maintain or tighten policy, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If signals fail to produce negative forward returns in at least 60% of triggered instances over 2-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate broad-based economic expansion that drives corporate revenue growth, supporting S&P 500 positive returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both exceed their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: If joint signal produces positive returns in less than 55% of instances, or if each series alone has higher predictive power, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment (FEDFUNDS >= 5%), declining housing starts presage a downturn in residential investment and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in high-rate regimes, declining HOUST does not lead to significant negative returns (t-test p>0.10) over 2-year rolling windows, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 performance over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 quarterly changes show zero or negative correlation with subsequent 60-day returns (Spearman rho < 0.1 over 5-year rolling window), hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp negative month-over-month change in total nonfarm payroll employment signals imminent economic contraction that rapidly feeds into equity markets as earnings downgrades hit, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If a negative PAYEMS reading is not followed by negative 5-day returns in >70% of occurrences over 3-year rolling window, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated (above 5%) and the unemployment rate is very low (below 4%), the economy is in a late-cycle phase where tightening risks a policy mistake, increasing recession probability and weighing on S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If joint condition does not lead to significantly negative average 20-day returns (one-sided t-test p>0.05) over 5-year period, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a recovery regime (characterized by declining unemployment from peaks), accelerating industrial production indicates that the recovery is gaining traction, supporting continued equity upside over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If during periods of declining UNRATE, rising INDPRO does not predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05, hypothesis rejected.
